channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding IELTS: Why Filipino Students and Workers Must Take It for Opportunities Abroad
item.description: The International English Language Testing System (IELTS) is an English proficiency exam widely recognized around the world. Many Filipinos encounter it when planning to study, work, or migrate abroad...

item.title: Lawyer Njeri Maina Refutes Violence Claims During Gachagua Impeachment
item.description: During a tense courtroom session on the impeachment of former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua, lawyer Jane Njeri Maina firmly defended her actions and dismissed allegations that she was involved in ...

item.title: Review: Stunning Visuals Overshadow Thin Story in 'The Mandalorian and Grogu'
item.description: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ offers a visually striking experience, but it struggles to deliver a compelling narrative. Pedro Pascal reprises his role from the TV series, embarking on a mission to secu...

item.title: Pakistan's CDF Asim Munir undertakes official visit to Iran for peace talks
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of the Army Staff Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir has departed for an official visit to Iran on Friday, according to security sources. During his visit, CDF Munir...

item.title: Guidelines for Using AI Effectively in Education
item.description: The use of AI in education often sparks debate among students and institutions. While some schools adopt a strict 'AI is bad' stance, many students find AI to be a valuable tool when used responsibly....

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Patients Accessing Serious Medical Results Via App Before Their Doctors
item.description: Physicians in Newfoundland and Labrador are raising concerns about a new health information app, MyChart, linked to the province's CorCare digital system, which allows patients to view their medical t...

item.title: UK Government Borrowing Surpasses Forecasts in April Amid Rising Costs
item.description: In April, the UK government's borrowing reached £24.3 billion, significantly exceeding both the figure from the same period last year and the £20.9 billion projected by the Office for Budget Responsib...

item.title: Tanzania Builds 1,604 Edible Oil Processing Facilities to Increase Local Production and Lower Imports
item.description: The Tanzanian government has taken big steps to strengthen the edible oil sector by setting up 1,604 processing industries across the country. This move aims at achieving self-sufficiency in cooking o...

item.title: Bilawal Bhutto opens Shahrah-i-Bhutto and lays foundation for new Karachi Port expressway
item.description: On Friday, PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari officially inaugurated the 39-kilometre-long Shahrah-i-Bhutto Expressway, which connects Qayyumabad in Karachi to the M-9 Motorway. This significant deve...

item.title: Rio Ferdinand Named Voluntary Tourism Ambassador to Promote Tanzania’s Natural Sites
item.description: Tanzania has officially appointed former England and Manchester United footballer, Rio Ferdinand, as a voluntary tourism ambassador. The announcement was made in Arusha where Dr. Ashatu Kijaji, the Mi...

item.title: Terror suspect linked to 2019 Pulwama attack killed in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir
item.description: Arjumand Gulzar, also known as Burhan Hamza, who was designated as a terrorist by the Indian government in 2022, has been reportedly killed in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir. Officials cited by PTI stated ...

item.title: Nova Scotia ski resort stores snow under thermal blankets to prepare for next season
item.description: Ski Martock, located about 60 kilometres northwest of Halifax, Nova Scotia, is using high-tech insulated blankets to preserve a large stockpile of snow for the upcoming ski season. The snow pile measu...

item.title: Cape Independence Advocacy Group requests US backing for Western Cape self-determination referendum
item.description: The Cape Independence Advocacy Group (CIAG) has written an open letter to US President Donald Trump, asking for American support for a referendum on self-determination in the Western Cape. This comes ...

item.title: How a Chance Encounter in a Subway Led to Raising a Son
item.description: In the summer of 2000, a New Yorker, aged 34, discovered a newborn baby abandoned at Union Square subway station. With the umbilical cord still attached, he quickly contacted emergency services and wa...

item.title: Tragic Boat Accident Claims Lives Of Pastor, Wife, And Church Members In Bayelsa
item.description: One kind wahala don happen for Bayelsa state as a boat accident don carry go the life of one serving Pastor, him wife, and some church members along Brass waterways. The gbege happen for Odioama, Bras...

item.title: Life and Leisure at the Seaside in the Early 20th Century
item.description: In the early 20th century, seaside vacations were far more formal and structured than modern beach trips. During the Belle Époque and Edwardian eras, adults dressed elegantly for the beach, with women...

item.title: Turkish Air Chief Appreciates Pakistan Air Force Support in Pilot Training During Official Visit
item.description: During an official visit to Turkiye, Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Babar Sidhu of Pakistan Air Force (PAF) met top Turkish officials to discuss enhanced collaboration in defence and aerospace sectors...

item.title: Gérard Albouy: Celebrated Mid-Century French Milliner and Artist
item.description: Gérard Albouy (1912–1985) was a pioneering French artist and milliner whose innovative approach transformed hat-making into an art form. Best known as part of the duo “Gaby et Gérard,” Albouy’s creati...

item.title: Global Investment Shifts Amid the 2025–2026 Middle East Conflict
item.description: The 2025–2026 period, particularly during the Israel-US-Iran conflict, has shown significant shifts in global investment patterns, highlighting the close link between geopolitics and capital flows. No...

item.title: Tanzania VP Calls for Professionalism and Integrity Among Personal Secretaries
item.description: Tanzania’s Vice-President Emmanuel Nchimbi has urged personal secretaries across the country to maintain high professionalism and discipline, highlighting their role as the first point of contact in b...
